tests.test_name: aldehydes ketones and carboxylic acids Test-01

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of CH 3 CHO is
questions.question_html: IUPAC name of CH3CHO is
questions.explanation: CH 3 CHO is ethanal as we have two carbons in the main chain so, the word root is 'Eth' and the functional group is an aldehydic group so, the suffix is 'al'. Hence, IUPAC name will be Ethanal.

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC names of the following compound: CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 CH(Br)CH(CH 3 )CH 2 CHO
questions.question_html: Give IUPAC names of the following compound: CH3CH2CH2CH(Br)CH(CH3)CH2CHO
questions.explanation: The IUPAC of the above compound will be more clear if we open up the structure: The structure of the above compounds is (longest chain contain 7 carbon) Hence the IUPAC name of the above compound is 4-Bromo-3-methylheptanal.

questions.question_text: Give the IUPAC name of the following compound: {tex}C{H_3} - C{H_2} - C{H_2}\mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{C{H_3}} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^O - H{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Give the IUPAC name of the following compound:
{tex}C{H_3} - C{H_2} - C{H_2}\mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{C{H_3}} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^O - H{/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}C{H_3} - C{H_2} - C{H_2}\mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{C{H_3}} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^O - H{/tex} Since functional group CHO is present so it is given first priority and consider a part of the main chain. Then the name of substituents is given first in alphabetical order followed by word root which is 'hept' and finally suffix which is'al'.

questions.question_html: Give IUPAC names of the following compound:
questions.explanation: here functional group is ketone so numbering will start from functional group. at 1 position phenyl group is attached so, 1-phenyl will be subsituent name written first, followed by word root which is 'prop' and finally suffix 'one'

questions.explanation: This is 4 - nitrobenzenecarbaldehyde and 4 - nitrobenzaldehyde. iupac name is 4 - nitrobenzaldehyde

questions.explanation: This is alpha Methoxypropionaldehyde (common name) and 2 – Methoxypropanal (IUPAC name).

questions.question_text: The carbonyl carbon atom
questions.question_html: The carbonyl carbon atom

options.html: forms a {tex}\pi{/tex} – bond with oxygen

questions.explanation: R 1 R 2 C=O is carbonyl so 3 sigma bond. two sigma bond with C chain attached (R1 and R2) and one sigma bond with the oxygen atom. and it also form1 pie bond with oxygen. the hybridisation of carbonyl carbon = sp 2 as it forms a total 3 sigma bond and has zero lone pair. if the number of sigma bond + lone pair = 3 then hybridisation is sp 2 .

questions.question_text: Prop – 2 – enal is called
questions.question_html: Prop – 2 – enal is called
questions.explanation: Acrolein is the common name of prop-2-enal.

questions.question_text: Ozonolysis of alkenes followed by the reaction with zinc dust and water gives ______ depending on the substitution pattern of the alkene.
questions.question_html: Ozonolysis of alkenes followed by the reaction with zinc dust and water gives ______ depending on the substitution pattern of the alkene.

options.text: a mixture of both aldehyde and ketone
options.html: a mixture of both aldehyde and ketone

questions.explanation: Reductive ozonolysis yields aldehyde or ketone depending on the nature of alkene. for example, CH 2 = CH 2 on reaction with O 3 /Zn will give 2 molecules of HCHO while CH 3 C(CH 3 ) = CHCH 3 on the reaction with O 3 /Zn will give one molecule of propanone and one molecule of acetaldehyde, and CH 3 C(CH 3 ) = C(CH 3 )CH 3 on reaction with O 3 /Zn will give 2 molecules of propanone.

questions.question_text: Esters react with DIBAL – H to produce
questions.question_html: Esters react with DIBAL – H to produce
questions.explanation: Esters are reduced to aldehydes selectively with DIBAL-H. DIBAL-H is bulky and electrophilic reducing agent.

questions.question_text: Aldehydes are produced on reduction of the following by DIBAL – H:
questions.question_html: Aldehydes are produced on reduction of the following by DIBAL – H:
questions.explanation: Both Nitriles and esters are reduced to Aldehyde on reaction with DIBAL-H. {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^{\ddot O:} - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} - C{H_2} - C{H_2}\xrightarrow[\begin{subarray}{l} 2.{H_2}O \end{subarray} ]{{1.DIBAL - H}}C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^{\ddot O:} H{/tex} {tex}C{H_3} - C \equiv N\xrightarrow[\begin{subarray}{l} 2.{H_2}O \end{subarray} ]{{1.DIBAL - H}}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^{\ddot O:} H{/tex}

questions.question_text: The oxidation of toluene to benzoic acid can be stopped at the aldehyde stage to give benzaldehyde. The reagent used for the purpose is one of the following.

questions.question_text: Aldehydes are prepared by reducing nitriles to corresponding imines with stannous chloride in the presence of hydrochloric acid. This reaction is called
questions.question_html: Aldehydes are prepared by reducing nitriles to corresponding imines with stannous chloride in the presence of hydrochloric acid. This reaction is called
questions.explanation: Reaction for preparation of aldehydes by reducing RCN in presence of SnCl 2 is Stephen's Reaction. The mechanism of the reaction is as shown below. SnCl 2 + 2HCl {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} SnCl 2 + 2[H] R - C {tex}\equiv{/tex} N + 2[H] + HCl {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} R - CH {tex}={/tex} NH.HCl {tex}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{Boiling \ H_2O}}{/tex} + NH 4 Cl

questions.question_text: The oxidation of toluene to benzoic acid can be done using which of the following reagents
questions.question_html: The oxidation of toluene to benzoic acid can be done using which of the following reagents
questions.explanation: Toulene can be converted directly to benzoic acid on its reaction with KMnO 4 , OH - and H 3 O + .

questions.question_text: Benzene gets converted to benzaldehyde by Gatterman – Koch's reaction. The appropriate reagent for the conversion is:
questions.question_html: Benzene gets converted to benzaldehyde by Gatterman – Koch's reaction. The appropriate reagent for the conversion is:
questions.explanation: Gatterman Koch is a kind of Friedel craft acylation done in presence of CO and HCl using AlCl 3 as a catalyst and is used to produce aromatic aldehydes. C 6 H 6 + CO + HCl {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} C 6 H 5 CHO

tests.test_name: aldehydes ketones and carboxylic acids Test-02

questions.question_text: Arrange the following sets of compounds in order of their increasing boiling points : Pentan – 1 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, methanol
questions.question_html:
Arrange the following sets of compounds in order of their increasing boiling points :
Pentan – 1 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, methanol

options.text: Butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol, methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol
options.html: Butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol, methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol

options.text: Methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol.
options.html: Methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol.

options.text: Pentan – 1 – ol Methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol
options.html: Pentan – 1 – ol Methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol

options.text: Propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol, methanol, ethanol
options.html: Propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol, methanol, ethanol

questions.correct_option_text: Methanol, ethanol, propan – 1 – ol, butan – 2 – ol, butan – 1 – ol, pentan – 1 – ol.
questions.explanation: Boiling point is α the Van der Waal forces α Molecular weight . When the molecular weight is the same as in the case of butan-1-ol and butan -2- ol, then the boiling point increases as branching decreases. This is so because, with an increase in branching, a molecule occupies a spherical shape, and surface area of contact decreases, and hence boiling point decreases. So, butan-2-ol will have a lower boiling point than butan-1-ol.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following alcohols, hydrocarbon, and ether in order of their increasing boiling points. Pentan–1–ol, n-butane, pentanal, ethoxyethane.
questions.question_html:
Arrange the following alcohols, hydrocarbon, and ether in order of their increasing boiling points.
Pentan–1–ol, n-butane, pentanal, ethoxyethane.

options.text: n – Butane, ethoxyethane, pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol
options.html: n – Butane, ethoxyethane, pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol

options.text: pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol, n – Butane, ethoxyethane
options.html: pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol, n – Butane, ethoxyethane

options.text: pentan – 1 – ol , n – Butane, ethoxyethane, pentanal
options.html: pentan – 1 – ol , n – Butane, ethoxyethane, pentanal

options.text: ethoxyethane, pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol, n – Butane
options.html: ethoxyethane, pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol, n – Butane

questions.correct_option_text: n – Butane, ethoxyethane, pentanal and pentan – 1 – ol
questions.explanation: Alcohols are involved in hydrogen bonding with each other i.e. intermolecular hydrogen bonding and thus have a higher boiling point compared to aldehydes or ketones and ethers. Aldehydes and ketones due to their polar CO bond have higher boiling point compared to ethers. Alkanes being non-polar have the least boiling point.

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of the following compound is: {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits_| }\limits_{C{H_3}} - OC{H_3}{/tex}

IUPAC name of the following compound is:
{tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits_| }\limits_{C{H_3}} - OC{H_3}{/tex}
questions.explanation: Ether is the functional group here and is given priority so it is present at position 1, and also at position 1 methyl substituent is present so the IUPAC name is 1-methoxy-1-methylethane.

questions.question_html: The reagents to bring about the change from but – 2 – ene to ethanal is:
questions.explanation: Reductive Ozonolysis of alkene forms aldehyde or ketone or their mixture depending on the type of alkene (but-2-ene) i.e. CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 + O 3 /Zn {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2CH 3 CHO.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is used for the separation and purification of aldehydes
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is used for the separation and purification of aldehydes
questions.explanation: Aldehydes and ketones form bisulphite addition product with NaHSO 3 while impurites does not on hydrolysis we get pure aldehydes and ketones.

questions.question_text: The aldehydes and ketones undergo one of the following reactions:
questions.question_html: The aldehydes and ketones undergo one of the following reactions:
questions.explanation: Aldehydes and Ketones undergo the characteristic nucleophilic addition reaction. this is because of the presence of polar>C=O bond in aldehydes and ketones. due to electronegativity difference between C and O, O bears a small (delta ) negative charge while C bears a small (delta) positive charge. now because of delta positive charge on C nucleophile can add to >C=O bond giving nucleophilic addition reaction.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following compounds in decreasing order of their acid strength: i) trichloroacetic acid ii) trifluoroacetic acid iii) acetic acid and iv)formic acid
questions.question_html: Arrange the following compounds in decreasing order of their acid strength: i) trichloroacetic acid ii) trifluoroacetic acid iii) acetic acid and iv)formic acid

options.text: trichloroacetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid, acetic acid and formic acid.
options.html: trichloroacetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid, acetic acid and formic acid.

options.text: trifluoroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, formic acid and acetic acid
options.html: trifluoroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, formic acid and acetic acid

options.text: trifluoroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, formic acid.and acetic acid
options.html: trifluoroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, formic acid.and acetic acid

options.text: trifluoroacetic acid, formic acid acetic acid and Propan – 1 – ol, 4 – methylphenol
options.html: trifluoroacetic acid, formic acid acetic acid and Propan – 1 – ol, 4 – methylphenol

questions.correct_option_text: trifluoroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, formic acid and acetic acid
questions.explanation: Stronger acid always has a stable conjugate base. If RCOOH is acid and its conjugate base is RCOO - , then the conjugate base of RCOOH will get stabilised when R group contains electron withdrawing group like F (strong -I effect). Stability of the conjugate base will increase the acidity of acid. While if R group is CH 3 which shows electron donation (+I effect), the conjugate base gets destabilised and acidity decreases.

questions.question_text: CH 3 CHO and C 6 H 5 CH 2 CHO can be distinguished chemically by:
questions.question_html: CH3CHO and C6H5CH2CHO can be distinguished chemically by:
questions.explanation: CH 3 CHO due to the presence of {tex}\left(CH_3-\overset O{\overset{\vert\vert}C}-H\right){/tex} group reacts with NaOH and I 2 to give yellow crystals of iodoform while C6H5CH2CHO does not react with it. CH 3 CHO + 3I 2 + 4NaOH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CHI 3 + HCOONa + 3NaI + 3H 2 O C 6 H 5 CH 2 CHO + I 2 + NaOH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} No reaction.

questions.question_text: Which of the following acids does not exhibit optical isomerism?
questions.question_html: Which of the following acids does not exhibit optical isomerism?
questions.explanation: Maleic Acid shows Geometrical Isomerism due to restricted bond rotation along with C=C bond but does not give optical isomerism as it has a horizontal plane of symmetry, as C=C bond is planar and thus do not form a non-superimposable mirror image and is optically inactive.

questions.question_text: The correct sequence of steps involved in the mechanism of Cannizzaro's reaction is
questions.question_html: The correct sequence of steps involved in the mechanism of Cannizzaro's reaction is

options.text: transfer of H + , nucleophilic attack and transfer of H –
options.html: transfer of H<sup>+</sup>, nucleophilic attack and transfer of H<sup>–</sup>

options.text: electrophilic attack by OH – , transfer of H -
options.html: electrophilic attack by OH<sup>–</sup>, transfer of H<sup>-</sup>

options.text: nucleophilic attack, transfer of H – and transfer of H +
options.html: nucleophilic attack, transfer of H<sup>–</sup> and transfer of H<sup>+</sup>

options.text: electrophilic attack by OH – , transfer of H + and transfer of H –
options.html: electrophilic attack by OH<sup>–</sup> , transfer of H<sup>+</sup> and transfer of H<sup>–</sup>

questions.correct_option_text: nucleophilic attack, transfer of H – and transfer of H +
questions.explanation: In Cannizzaro reaction 1st nucleophile OH - attacks on carbonyl carbon. Then hydride shifting takes place. Followed by proton transfer as shown below ( in, RCHO, R group has no alpha hydrogen) : Cannizaro is a kind of disproportionation reaction where aldehyde or ketones having no alpha hydrogen get oxidised to acid and reduced to alcohol.

questions.question_text: The compound obtained when acetaldehyde reacts with dilute aqueous sodium hydroxide exhibits:
questions.question_html: The compound obtained when acetaldehyde reacts with dilute aqueous sodium hydroxide exhibits:
questions.explanation: When acetaldehyde is treated with dilute aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, it undergoes aldol condensation (because of the presence of ? -H atom) as. {tex}\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO}+\mathrm{HCH}_2 \mathrm{CHO} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{NaOH}]{\text { Dil.aqueous }}{/tex} {tex}\begin{gathered} \mathrm{CH}_3 \stackrel{\star}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CHO} \\ \text { aldol } \\ \text { (shows optical isomerism) } \end{gathered}{/tex} Since, the aldol formed contains one asymmetric carbon atom, it will show optical isomerism. On heating, aldol loses water and gives an alkene. {tex} {CH}_3 {CH}({OH}) {CH}_2 {CHO} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { (shows geometrical isomerism) }}{{CH}_3 {CH}={CHCHO}}{/tex} The obtained alkene shows geometrical isomerism.

questions.question_text: Benzaldehyde and acetone can be best distinguished by using:
questions.question_html: Benzaldehyde and acetone can be best distinguished by using:
questions.explanation: Tollen's Test is used to distinguish between aldehyde and ketone. It uses the fact that aldehydes are easily oxidised to their corresponding acids while ketones are not. Tollen's reagent is aqueous ammonical silver nitrate solution which reacts with aldehydes as shown. RCHO + 2Ag + + 2OH - {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCOO - + Ag + H 2 O. RCOR + 2Ag + + 2OH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} No reaction If this test is carried in a glass tube, the Ag formed forms a mirror on the sides of the test tube so the test is also known as the silver mirror test. Aldehydes show Tollen’s test while acetone which is a ketone does not give Tollen’s test.

questions.question_text: Acetone is treated with excess of ethanol in the presence of hydrochloric acid. The product obtained is:
questions.question_html: Acetone is treated with excess of ethanol in the presence of hydrochloric acid. The product obtained is:
questions.explanation: Ketones or aldehydes react with alcohols to form acetals. This reaction of alcohol on aldehydes or ketones is catalyzed in the presence of acid and is a reversible reaction. Firstly a hemiacetal (CH 3 ) 2 C(OH)(OC 2 H 5 ) is formed which further reacts with alcohol to give acetal. (CH 3 ) 2 C(OC 2 H 5 )(OC 2 H 5 ) CH 3 COCH 3 + 2C 2 H 5 OH {tex}\overset{H^+}\rightleftarrows{/tex} (CH 3 ) 2 C(OC 2 H 5 )(OC 2 H 5 )

questions.question_text: Give the products of the reaction {tex}PhC \equiv CMe\xrightarrow{{{H_3}{O^ + },H{g^{2 + }}}}?{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Give the products of the reaction
{tex}PhC \equiv CMe\xrightarrow{{{H_3}{O^ + },H{g^{2 + }}}}?{/tex}
questions.explanation: Hydration of given alkyne will occur according to Markovnikoff's rule leading to the formation of enol which will tautomerise to give keto form. The general reaction of alkyne hydrations shown below: Same reaction will occur with {tex}PhC \equiv CMe\xrightarrow{{{H_3}{O^ + },H{g^{2 + }}}}?{/tex} {tex}PhC(OH) = CHC{H_3} \Leftrightarrow PhCOC{H_2}C{H_3}{/tex} .

questions.question_text: The incorrect IUPAC name is:
questions.question_html: The incorrect IUPAC name is:
questions.explanation: Numbering is done in such a way that CO gets the lower number as CO is the functional group and should get the first priority. The correct IUPAC name of the compound should be 3-methylbut-2-one.

questions.question_text: One mole of a symmetrical alkane on ozonolysis gives two moles of an aldehyde having a molecular mass of 44u. The alkene is:
questions.question_html: One mole of a symmetrical alkane on ozonolysis gives two moles of an aldehyde having a molecular mass of 44u. The alkene is:
questions.explanation: 2-butene on reductive ozonolysis with O 3 /Zn will give CH 3 CHO which has a molecular mass of 44u. CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 + O 3 /Zn {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2CH 3 CHO Molecular mass of CH 3 CHO = 12 + 3 + 12 + 1 + 16 = 44u

questions.question_text: The compound formed as a result of oxidation of ethyl benzene by KMnO 4 is
questions.question_html: The compound formed as a result of oxidation of ethyl benzene by KMnO4 is
questions.explanation: Alkylbenzenes having at least one alpha hydrogen when reacts with KMnO 4 then, alkyl group oxidises to COOH group. C 6 H 5 C 2 H 5 + KMnO 4 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} C 6 H 5 COOH

questions.question_text: Ketones are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by catalytic hydrogenation to form_____.
questions.question_html: Ketones are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by catalytic hydrogenation to form_____.
questions.explanation: Aldehydes on catalytic hydrogenation using H 2 /Pt give primary alcohols while ketones on catalytic hydrogenation using H 2 /Pt give secondary alcohols.

questions.question_html: Which of the following given reaction is correct?

options.html: Sucrose on reaction with NaCl give invert sugar.

options.text: Reaction of vegetable oil with H 2 SO 4 gives glycerine.
options.html: Reaction of vegetable oil with H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> gives glycerine.

options.text: Any aldehyde gives secondary alcohol on reduction.
options.html: Any aldehyde gives secondary alcohol on reduction.

options.text: C 2 H 5 OH, iodine with NaOH gives iodoform.
options.html: C<sub>2</sub>H<sub>5</sub>OH, iodine with NaOH gives iodoform.

questions.correct_option_text: C 2 H 5 OH, iodine with NaOH gives iodoform.
questions.explanation: I 2 in the presence of NaOH act as a weak oxidizing agent and thus C 2 H 5 OH gets oxidizes to CH 3 CHO and COCH 3 group is important for iodoform. CH 3 CHO + I 2 + OH - {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CHI 3 + HCOO - CHI 3 is a yellow ppt known as iodoform.

questions.question_text: Reduction of aldehydes and ketones into hydrocarbons using zinc amalgam and conc. HCl is called:
questions.question_html: Reduction of aldehydes and ketones into hydrocarbons using zinc amalgam and conc. HCl is called:
questions.explanation: Clemmensen reduction: Aldehydes and ketones are reduced to the corresponding alkanes by means of amalgamated Zinc and HCl.

questions.question_text: The appropriate reagent for the transformation:
questions.question_html: The appropriate reagent for the transformation:
questions.explanation: For conversion of the carbonyl group (>CO) to alkane group (-CH 2 ) two types of reduction reactions are there: Clemmson Reduction (Zn-Hg , conc HCl) Wolff Kishner ( NH 2 -NH 2 , conc base ) Since Clemson reduction is done in acidic medium using HCl. So, if any acid sensitive group like -OH is present Clemmson reduction will not be a suitable reduction process. Cl - of HCl will react with OH group and will substitute OH with Cl group. For the following conversion: since -OH group is present, clemmson reduction will not be a suitable reduction hence wolff kishner reduction should be used.

questions.question_text: A mixture of benzaldehyde and formaldehyde on heating with aqueous NaOH solution gives:
questions.question_html: A mixture of benzaldehyde and formaldehyde on heating with aqueous NaOH solution gives:
questions.explanation: They will undergo Cannizzaro reaction as neither benzaldehyde nor formaldehyde has alpha hydrogen. HCHO will be more reactive towards Cannizzaro compared to benzaldehyde because of less steric hindrance. So, OH - nucleophile will attack HCHO first, and then the hydride shift from HCHO to benzaldehyde will occur and thus HCHO will oxidize to HCOO - ion and benzaldehyde will reduce to benzyl alcohol.

questions.question_text: Which of the following has most acidic hydrogen?
questions.question_html: Which of the following has most acidic hydrogen?
questions.explanation: 2,4-hexanedione will have active methylene group. The structure of 2,4-hexanedione is -CH 2 group present between the two carbonyl group is active methylene group, these hydrogens are highly acidic as their conjugate base is highly stable.

questions.question_text: Clemmensen reduction of a ketone is carried out in the presence of which of the following?
questions.question_html: Clemmensen reduction of a ketone is carried out in the presence of which of the following?
questions.explanation: This is called as clemmensen reduction.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is not correct?
questions.question_html: Which of the following statements is not correct?

options.text: Lower members of aldehydes and ketones are soluble in water due to hydrogen bonding.
options.html: Lower members of aldehydes and ketones are soluble in water due to hydrogen bonding.

options.text: Aldehydes and ketones undergo electrophilic substitution.
options.html: Aldehydes and ketones undergo electrophilic substitution.

options.text: Aldehydes and ketones undergo nucleophilic addition.
options.html: Aldehydes and ketones undergo nucleophilic addition.

options.text: Aldehydes and ketones contain polar carbonyl group.
options.html: Aldehydes and ketones contain polar carbonyl group.

questions.correct_option_text: Aldehydes and ketones undergo electrophilic substitution.
questions.explanation: Aldehydes and ketones have polar C=O group therefore they undergo nucleophilic addition reactions. The oxygen being electronegative have a delta (small) negative charge and thus C attached to oxygen bears a positive charge. Thus this electrophilic C attracts a nucleophile to add to its double bond. Thus aldehydes and ketones undergo nucleophilic addition reactions.

questions.question_text: In Hell – Volhard Zelinsky reaction, halogen reacts with
questions.question_html: In Hell – Volhard Zelinsky reaction, halogen reacts with
questions.explanation: Alpha Hydrogen containing carboxylic acids undergo HVZ reaction. HVZ reaction is used for alpha halogenation of carboxylic acid.

questions.question_text: In Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky reaction, the reagents used are:
questions.question_html: In Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky reaction, the reagents used are:

options.text: Br 2 in the presence of CHCl 3
options.html: Br<sub>2</sub> in the presence of CHCl<sub>3</sub>

options.text: Cl 2 in the presence of NaOH
options.html: Cl<sub>2</sub> in the presence of NaOH

options.text: Br 2 in the presence of red P 4

questions.correct_option_text: Br 2 in the presence of red P 4
questions.explanation: In HVZ we use Br 2 in the presence of Red P 4 . Phosphorus reacts with bromine to give phosphorus tribromide, and this converts the carboxylic acid into an acyl bromide.

questions.question_text: The following reaction is:
questions.question_html: The following reaction is:
questions.explanation: This is Wolff Kishner reduction of Carbonyls to alkanes. Wolff kishner reaction uses hydrazine ( NH 2 -NH 2 ) and conc base like NaOH or KOH for reduction of carbonyl to alkanes.

questions.question_text: Compound 'A' undergoes the formation of cyanohydrins which on hydrolysis gives lactic acid (CH 3 CHOHCOOH). Therefore, compound 'A' is:
questions.question_html: Compound 'A' undergoes the formation of cyanohydrins which on hydrolysis gives lactic acid (CH3CHOHCOOH). Therefore, compound 'A' is:
questions.explanation: Cyanohydrin formed from aldehydes or ketones followed by complete hydrolysis that is used to produce alpha hydroxycarboxylic acids and on complete hydrolysis, CN converts to COOH. The complete reaction as follows: CH 3 CHO + HCN {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH(OH)CN CH 3 CH(OH)CN + H 3 O + {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH(OH)COOH

tests.test_name: aldehydes ketones and carboxylic acids Test-04

questions.question_html: Which sequence of steps below describes the best synthesis of 5 – oxohexanoic acid starting with 1 – methylcyclopentan – 1 – ol.

options.text: Conc. KMnO 4 CH 3 MgBr/ether H 3 O +
Conc. KMnO 4 Dry gaseous HBr mg/ether CO 2
H 2 SO 4 and heat O 3 (CH 3 ) 2 S PCC
H 2 SO 4 and heat Conc. KMnO 4

questions.correct_option_text: H 2 SO 4 and heat Conc. KMnO 4
questions.explanation: 5 – oxohexanoic acid starting with 1 – methylcyclopentan – 1 – ol can be synthesized using conc H 2 SO 4 which will cause dehydration forming alkene which with KMnO 4 opens the ring and forms 1 – methylcyclopentan – 1 – ol.​​​​​​​ this 1-methylcyclopentan-1-ol reacts with conc H 2 SO 4 to form 1-methylcyclopent-1-ene which on reaction with conc KMnO 4 cause oxidative ozonolysis of alkene and forms 5 – oxohexanoic acid. ​​​​​​​

questions.question_text: The compound which forms acetaldehyde when heated with dilute NaOH is:
questions.question_html: The compound which forms acetaldehyde when heated with dilute NaOH is:
questions.explanation: CH 3 CHCl 2 + OH - {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH(OH) 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CHO + H 2 O Gem diols like (CH 3 CH(OH) 2 ) are generally not stable. The 2 -OH group attached to the same C removes H 2 O and forms carbonyl compounds.

questions.question_text: For making a distinction between 2 – pentanone and 3 – pentanone the reagent to be employed is:
questions.question_html: For making a distinction between 2 – pentanone and 3 – pentanone the reagent to be employed is:
questions.explanation: 2-pentanone ( CH 3 COCH 2 CH 2 CH 3 ) will give an iodoform test (reaction with I 2 + NaOH ) because of the presence CH 3 CO- group and yellow precipitate will be formed. But 3-pentanone (CH 3 CH 2 COCH 2 CH 3 ) does not have CH 3 CO- group hence will not give the iodoform test. The reaction is as follows: CH 3 COCH 2 CH 2 CH 3 + I 2 + NaOH {tex}\rightarrow {/tex} CHI 3 (yellow precipitate) + CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 COO - Na + CH 3 CH 2 COCH 2 CH 3 + I 2 + NaOH {tex}\rightarrow {/tex} No reaction

questions.question_html: A strong base can abstract an {tex}\alpha{/tex} – hydrogen from
questions.explanation: Conjugate base of ketone which is formed is stable due to electron with drawing nature of adjacent CO group and resonance.

questions.question_text: The product formed in Aldol condensation is:
questions.question_html: The product formed in Aldol condensation is:

options.text: a beta – hydroxy aldehyde or a beta – hydroxy ketone.
options.html: a beta – hydroxy aldehyde or a beta – hydroxy ketone.

options.text: an alpha – hydroxy aldehyde or ketone.
options.html: an alpha – hydroxy aldehyde or ketone.

questions.correct_option_text: a beta – hydroxy aldehyde or a beta – hydroxy ketone.
questions.explanation: In aldol condensation when both molecules are same then it is called as self aldol condensation. e.g., {tex}\mathrm H-\overset{\mathrm H}{\underset{\mathrm H}{\overset\vert{\underset\vert{\mathrm C}}}}-\overset{\mathrm O}{\overset{\vert\vert}{\mathrm C}}-\mathrm H+\mathrm H-\overset{\mathrm H}{\underset{\mathrm H}{\overset\vert{\underset\vert{\mathrm C}}}}-\overset{\mathrm O}{\overset{\vert\vert}{\mathrm C}}-\mathrm H{/tex} {tex}\stackrel{-\mathrm{OH}}{\rightleftharpoons} {/tex} {tex}\underset{3-\mathrm{hydroxy}\;\mathrm{butanal}\;\mathrm{or}\\\mathrm\beta-\mathrm{hydroxy}\;\mathrm{aldehyde}}{{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{\underset{\mathrm H}\vert\;\;}{{\overset{\mathrm H}{\overset\vert{\mathrm C}}}_{\mathrm\alpha}}-{\underset{\underset{\mathrm H}{\vert\;}\;\;}{{\overset{\mathrm H}{\overset\vert{\mathrm C}}}_{\mathrm\beta}}}_{}-\overset{\mathrm O}{\overset{\vert\vert}{\mathrm C}}-\mathrm H}{/tex}

questions.question_text: Which of the following reagents can be used to convert a carboxylic acid directly into its corresponding acid chloride derivative?
questions.question_html: Which of the following reagents can be used to convert a carboxylic acid directly into its corresponding acid chloride derivative?
questions.explanation: The acid reacts with SOCl 2 or (COCl) 2 and gets converted to acid chloride. With (COCl) 2 , room temperature is required. RCOOH + (COCl) 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCOCl

questions.question_text: What compound is produced when cyclohexene is treated with concentrated KMnO 4 ?
questions.question_html: What compound is produced when cyclohexene is treated with concentrated KMnO4?
questions.explanation: Conc. KMnO 4 will cause oxidative ozonolysis and ring-opening forming adipic acid. Cyclohexene + conc. KMnO 4 {tex}\rightarrow {/tex} HOOC(CH 2 ) 4 COOH

questions.question_text: How to do the following conversion: RCOOCl + ? {tex}\rightarrow {/tex} RCHO
questions.question_html:
How  to do the following conversion:
RCOOCl + ?  {tex}\rightarrow {/tex} RCHO
questions.explanation: Catalytic hydrogenation of acid chloride using H 2 -Pd, BaSO 4 converts acid chloride selectively to aldehydes ( BaSO 4 reduces the activity of palladium). This is known as Rosenmund Reduction.

questions.question_text: The common name for pentanedioic acid is:
questions.question_html: The common name for pentanedioic acid is:
questions.explanation: Pentanedioic acid is known as glutaric acid. (COOH) 2 is known as oxalic acid. Butanedioic acid is known as succinic acid. Hexanedioic acid is known as adipic acid. Heptanedioic acid is known as pimelic acid.

questions.question_text: What compound is produced when (CH 3 ) 2 CHCH 2 Br is subjected to the following sequence of steps: Mg, Et 2 O, CO 2 , H 3 O + ?
questions.question_html:
What compound is produced when (CH3)2 CHCH2Br is subjected to the following sequence of steps:

  1. Mg, Et2O,
  2. CO2,
  3. H3O+?
questions.explanation: Firstly, alkyl bromide will react with Mg/ether to form Grignard reagent (CH 3 ) 2 CHCH 2 Br + Mg/ether {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} (CH 3 ) 2 CHCH 2 MgBr Now Grignard reagent formes will act as a nucleophile and attack O=C=O, followed by hydrolysis will form acid. (CH 3 ) 2 CHCH 2 MgBr + CO 2 + H 3 O + {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} (CH 3 ) 2 CHCH 2 COOH The general reaction of grignard (CH 3 MgX) with CO 2 is as shown: Carbon dioxide {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Carboxylic acids

questions.question_text: Many naturally occurring aldehydes and ketones are used in the blending of perfumes and flavouring agents. But the preferred ones are______.
questions.question_html: Many naturally occurring aldehydes and ketones are used in the blending of perfumes and flavouring agents. But the preferred ones are______.
questions.explanation: Higher aldehydes like aldehyde C-10 etc. are used in floral blends like rose, jasmine, etc.

questions.question_text: The fragrance of the aldehyde and ketone are used for perfume and similar uses depend on:
questions.question_html: The fragrance of the aldehyde and ketone are used for perfume and similar uses depend on:

options.text: size and solubility of the aldehyde and ketone molecule.
options.html: size and solubility of the aldehyde and ketone molecule.

options.text: on its reactivity with other functional groups.
options.html: on its reactivity with other functional groups.

options.text: only solubility of aldehydes and ketones.
options.html: only solubility of aldehydes and ketones.

questions.correct_option_text: size and solubility of the aldehyde and ketone molecule.
questions.explanation: Size and solubility of aldehyde and ketone determine fragrance. For example, aldehyde C-10 is used in floral blends like rose, jasmine, etc. While aldehyde C-11 has a strong citrus smell and aldehyde C-16 has a strong strawberry smell.

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of CH 3 CHO is
questions.question_html: IUPAC name of CH3CHO is
questions.explanation: CH 3 CHO is ethanal as we have two carbons in the main chain so, the word root is 'Eth' and the functional group is an aldehydic group so, the suffix is 'al'. Hence, IUPAC name will be Ethanal.

questions.question_text: Benzaldehyde and acetone can be best distinguished by using:
questions.question_html: Benzaldehyde and acetone can be best distinguished by using:
questions.explanation: Tollen's Test is used to distinguish between aldehyde and ketone. It uses the fact that aldehydes are easily oxidised to their corresponding acids while ketones are not. Tollen's reagent is aqueous ammonical silver nitrate solution which reacts with aldehydes as shown. RCHO + 2Ag + + 2OH - {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCOO - + Ag + H 2 O. RCOR + 2Ag + + 2OH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} No reaction If this test is carried in a glass tube, the Ag formed forms a mirror on the sides of the test tube so the test is also known as the silver mirror test. Aldehydes show Tollen’s test while acetone which is a ketone does not give Tollen’s test.
